title: fix: repair media documentation links
author: ElliotEtag
pr_body_excerpt:
- Makes generated curl examples honor OpenAPI server precedence: operation, path, then root.
- Uses `media.pollinations.ai` for upload and retrieval examples instead of nonexistent gen routes.
- Corrects retention wording: duplicate uploads reset retention rather than becoming no-ops.
- Replaces three expired BYOP screenshots with current, verified media uploads.
- Adds validation that rejects media examples using the wrong origin.

Checks:
- `npm run docs:generate`
- `npm run docs:validate`
- `npm run typecheck`
- `npx vitest run test/docs.test.ts`
- New BYOP media URLs return `200 image/png`

Note: `lefthook` is unavailable locally; commits completed successfully.

gist.headline: Pointing in the right direction
gist.blurb: We've polished up the API documentation for media uploads. Curl examples now point to the right servers, and we've refreshed the screenshots so everything looks as good as it works.
gist.summary: Updated the API documentation generator to correctly route media upload and retrieval curl examples to media.pollinations.ai instead of the text generation endpoints.
gist.impact: Developers copying curl commands from the docs will now hit the correct media endpoints on the first try, and duplicate upload retention rules are now clearly explained.
gist.visual_concept: Polly the bee fixing a directional signpost to point the right way, while the Monitor Robot holds up a fresh, working picture (representing the fixed screenshots).
gist.image_prompt: Cozy pixel art scene of the cute bee mascot wearing a tiny tool belt, fixing a glowing lime green (#ecf874) wooden signpost that reads 'MEDIA'. A chunky robot with a CRT monitor head stands nearby holding a fresh, colorful picture frame. Soft pastel lavender and cream gradient background with warm lighting. Lo-fi 8-bit aesthetic, Stardew Valley vibes, emotionally warm and nostalgic.
